relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul turns to spiritual gifts so the community will not be unaware.
Former idol-leading is contrasted with Spirit-led confession.
True Spirit speech honors Jesus as Lord.
Many gifts, one Spirit.
Many services, one Lord.
Many workings, one Divine working all in all.
Spirit manifestation is given for shared benefit.
Wisdom and knowledge arise through the same Spirit.
Trust and healings are Spirit-gifts.
Powers, prophecy, spirit-discernment, tongues, and interpretation are distributed.
The one Spirit gives to each as Spirit wills.
One body with many members reveals Messiah.
One Spirit baptizes Judean, Greek, enslaved, and free into one body.
The body is many, not one isolated member.
The foot’s insecurity does not remove it from the body.
The ear’s difference does not exile it from belonging.
A body of only eye or only ear would lose its fullness.
The Divine places each member as willed.
One member alone cannot be body.
Many members, one body.
No member may say to another, I do not need you.
The weaker-seeming members are necessary.
Less honored parts receive more abundant honor.
The Divine blends the body by honoring what lacks.
The goal is no split, but equal care.
One suffering is shared suffering; one glory is shared joy.
You are Messiah’s body, each a member in part.
The assembly receives many ordered gifts and functions.
Not everyone has the same role.
Not every gift appears in every member.
Desire the greater gifts, but a surpassing way is about to open.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 12 is sacred body medicine: difference is not threat, weakness is necessary, and Spirit distributes gifts for the benefit of all.
